steps.title: Identify what would support the researcher's claim
steps.content: The passage contrasts a general declining trend with the researcher's claim: “some lakes have instead seen an increase in the duration of ice.” For data to support this, the table must show a specific lake with more ice days in 2005-06 than in 1980-81.

steps.title: Find the evidence of increase
steps.content: Choice A describes Lake Baikal having fewer days in 1980-81 than in 2005-06—which is an increase, exactly matching the researcher's claim. Choice C shows a decrease (the opposite), while B and D make comparisons between two different lakes without demonstrating any lake's change over time. That matches choice A — A is correct.
